titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Woolman Brush Papers
titlestmt.author: Abi Mason and Elizabeth Peters

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday February 6, 2020 at 23:00

did.abstract:
The John Woolman Brush papers consist of materials documented and compiled by John Brush himself throughout his career. The collection spans from Brush's early life after high school in 1914 through his retirement, with the latest materials being comments from the late 1970's in some of his earlier journals, and letters from the 1980's.
The collection is split into five series: Correspondence, Educational Materials, Pastoral Materials, Writings, and Journals and Diaries.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open to research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from Andover Newton Theological School, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged into five series: Correspondence, Educational Materials, Pastoral Materials, Writings, and Journals. J.W. Brush's correspondence is organized by year. The educational materials are organized into three subseries: first, J.W.B.'s student notebooks from Colby College, which are organized by term ("Student Materials"), Brush's materials for preparation to teach ("Course Materials"), and Brush's lectures and papers from throughout his career ("Lectures and Papers"). The pastoral materials include subseries of Liturgy and Worship, Pastoral Records, and Sermons. Brush's writings include Publications, Church Pamphlets (used in Brush's ministry and distributed further), and Addresses. Lastly, Brush's journals are comprised of three subseries: Personal Journals, Reading Notes, and Diaries, all of which are organized by year.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Woolman Brush was a scholar, professor, poet, writer, father, and husband during his 92 years of life. He was born on December 1, 1898 in Mount Vernon, NY. After spending his childhood in New York, Brush went to Colby College in Maine to study theology, where he graduated in 1920. Brush received his B.D. from Newton Theological Institution in January 18, 1924. Brush began his first ministry in Portland, Maine, at Stroudwater Baptist Church in 1923, and left shortly after he was ordained in 1924 as a Baptist minister. During this time, Brush met and married his wife Hilda. They moved to New Haven, CT in 1925 so that Brush could become the minister at First Baptist Church of New Haven. In 1934, Brush and his family moved back to Maine where he served as a minister at the First Baptist Church in Waterville until 1939. While finishing up his Ph.D. at Yale University in 1940-1941, Brush worked as an instructor of Church History. In 1942 he earned his Ph.D. and began working at Andover Newton Theological Seminary until 1964. Late in 1960, Brush became dangerously ill and was hospitalized for a brief period of time before being released and recovering to live out the rest of his life. Around his retirement in 1964, Brush was awarded Professor Emeritus at Andover Newton. After his tenure teaching, Brush continued his lifelong ventures of documenting his life in writing, traveling, and advancing his theological pursuits by writing sermons, preaching, and giving addresses for various events.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Woolman Brush papers (RG 283), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The John Woolman Brush papers consist of materials documented and compiled by John Brush himself throughout his career. The collection spans from Brush's early life after high school in 1914 through his retirement, with the latest materials being comments from the late 1970's in some of his earlier journals, and letters from the 1980's.
The collection is split into five series: Correspondence, Educational Materials, Pastoral Materials, Writings, and Journals and Diaries.

John Brush's correspondence spans over 60 years, from 1914 through the 1970's, and includes letters from mentors, publishers, professors, his wife Hilda, his children, and many of his friends and colleagues. In these letters, Brush discusses his own work and others', including the work of his Ph.D. students over the years. He consistently kept in touch with his mentors, and his collection of letters follows their lives and includes letters he himself sent. His letters discuss his personal life, politics, war, and other current events and topics relevant to his personal and professional theology. There is a surge of letters from late 1960 during his illness and hospitalization, and they continue into early 1961. The letters in the early years of his life and after this hospitalization are fewer, but there is at least one letter from almost every year between 1914 and 1989.

Brush's Educational Materials include his student notebooks, course materials, and lectures and papers. His student notebooks include courses about the book of Jeremiah, the psychology of religion, the life of Paul, church history (in several volumes), and homiletics. It seems Brush did not keep his notebooks from his graduate coursework, although he did keep many of his reading notes in journals from throughout the years (which are in the final series of this collection). He did keep intricate notes for the courses he taught and copies of the lectures he gave as a lecturer and professor, and he also kept many syllabi from his courses on church history.

The third series in this collection, Pastoral Materials, consists of liturgy and worship materials, church records, and sermons. "Liturgy and Worship" includes church bulletins from a church in California from Brush's close friend Shozo Hashimoto, a copy of a Reformation Sunday issue of the Andover Newton Bulletin, a prayer journal, one of Brush's personal bibles, and more. The pastoral records he kept include lists of baptisms, deaths, marriages he ordained, and other reports of committees Brush participated in during his career. The last subseries in this collection, Brush's sermons, include numerous sermons of varying topics. There are hundreds of sermons, each with a unique title and based on specific Bible passages. Many of these sermons were written in lettered series and kept organized, an order not disturbed in the archival process. One particular sermon of interest is a sermon Brush wrote against the KKK in 1924 when the local klan donated books about the problems of Roman Catholicism to his church, Glenwood Chapel, and Brush refused them. Brush's sermon outlines the innate problem of otherness and discredits the KKK's polemic against Catholics. The sermon was met with a negative response by the KKK published in a local newspaper, and this article is also included in this archive.

John Brush's Writings are split into three subseries: Publications, Church Pamphlets, and Addresses. His publications, although short, are several. He also wrote a work entitled Stroudwater Days, which was intended to be published as a collection of journals written by Brush describing his time working in his church. Brush's church pamphlets are about prayer, living a Protestant life, church history, and more. The final section of the Writings are Brush's Addresses, which consist of graduation speeches, faculty forum talks, and various others.

Brush's Journals and Diaries are plentiful, thorough, thought-provoking, informational, and detailed. He includes drawings and paintings, poetry both in dedicated volumes and sporadically, and quotes lined up on the inside covers of most of the journals. He has journals throughout his life, and diaries that can be paired up nearly every year with lists of daily events, to-dos, and daily jottings. His journals include reflections on Ghandi, the first televised presidential debate, the birth of Israel as a state, the atom bombs in World War II, the invasion of Poland by Hitler, addresses given by Winston Churchill, and so much more. Brush kept himself up to date on all current events, and his journal entries give a detailed account of living through each event. He also glued in newspaper articles, had entire journals dedicated to notetaking the philosophy and theology books he read, and reflected on many of his domestic and international travels. As the most personally and professionally telling of the materials in this collection, John Brush's collection of journals and diaries draw an intimate portrait of his own mind and the transformation of thought and politics throughout the decades.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John W. Brush kept intricate records of all his correspondence, and also kept hundreds of letters sent to him over the course of his life. Throughout this series, Brush's records and letters give insight into his family, friends, academic relationships, and career colleagues from college until retirement. This correspondence includes letters from publishers, colleagues and students, and his children. Although the volume per year is sporadic, there is a surge in late 1960 and early 1961 during the time Brush was sick and hospitalized. Brush survived this illness and went on to live decades longer, and his correspondence extends into the 1980's.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is organized by year, but for a detailed list of correspondence and/or to search for correspondence from a certain person see the New Correspondence Inventory in Box 1, Folder 2.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John W. Brush kept most of his undergraduate notebooks from courses he took at Colby College in the 1910's. This coursework was with professors Brush went on to have lifelong relationships with like W. Bradbury, G.E. Horr, and J.P. Berkeley. These materials are in the first subseries, Brush's Student materials. The second and third subseries, Course Materials and Lectures and Papers, include Brush's prep materials and lectures used for courses he taught later in his own career as a scholar and professor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Analysis of Romans IV (May 19, 1922); Evolution and Christianity (1921); Francis Thompson and the Hound of Heaven (May 1923); Henry Drummond (April 1923); The Meaning of Jesus' Death in our Salvation (May 16, 1922); Review of Loisy and Marti on the Religion of Israel (Dec. 1921); St. Francis of Assisi (Oct. 26, 1922); The Ultimate Basis of Faith in Jesus (Feb. 7, 1922); Woodrow Wilson: A Memorial (Feb. 10, 1924)

Pilgrim Series: Shoes of a Pilgrim (manuscript)

Pilgrim Series: To Go or to Stay
Pilgrim Series: Pilgrims of Various Sorts (manuscript)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush's Pastoral Materials consists of Liturgy and Worship Materials, Church Records, and Sermons. These files include church bulletins, records of births, deaths, and marriages, and various sermons and sermon series.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: In the third subseries, Sermons, Brush's sermons are arranged in an order set out by J.W.B. himself. Some are in no order but separated by sermon title, some with multiple versions or additions, and others are organized by lettered series (Series A Sermon Notes, etc.). The lettered series are in alphabet order, which is generally in order by date.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From: Glenwood Square Men's Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sermon given in response to a book donation by the KK to his church, Glenwood Chapel, which discussed the problems of Roman Catholics in particular. Article clippings and photos about/of the event are included. This includes a response directly from the KKK printed in a local newspaper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Living Truth (John 1:40-45); Jesus and Peter (Luke 22:54-62); July 4, 1922 (Romans 8:19); Jesus' Self-Assertion of 'I am the Light of the Moved'; The Gospel of the Grace of God (Acts 20:24); 'I Believe in the Forgiveness of Sins': CREED; The Presence of God (Acts 17:27, Psalm 139); 'Sic Transit Gloria Mundi' (I Corinthians 3:10-15); 'A Bruised need he will not break'; "Behold them the goodness and severity of God" (Romans 11:22); The Sharp Contrasts of Life; Trusting a Man (Matthew 11: 18-19)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Random sermons through senior year at seminary.' Includes: My People (Jeremiah 8:21); The Walk with God; 'He is our Peace' (Ephesians 2:14); Romans I.X; The Hard Sayings of Christ; Homage to Christ (A Palm Sunday Sermon); Treasuring the Blessed Memories of the Past (Psalm 137)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Portland Sermons: E 1-48." Includes: Rich Through Giving; The Divided Life (Romans 7:17-25); "Where the Road is Smooth" (Romans 7:13); The Sword of Christ and the Angel's Song (Luke 2, Matthew 10); Christ and the Children (Mark X:13-16); The Hidden Springs (Proverbs 4:23); The Crooked Straight (Isaiah 40:4); Holman Hunt's "The Light of the World" (Revelation 3:20, John 8:12; II Corinthians 4:6); The Fellowship that will Redeem the world; Complete Consecration (John 13: 9); Seeking the Eternal Strength; "I will guide thee with mine eye..." (Psalms); Fireside Talk to the Young People; If Thine Eye Be Single (Matthew 6:22); The Democracy of Christianity; The Consolations of God (Job 15:11); Following the Gleam (Jeremiah 29:13); What Shall We Believe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Portland Sermons: F 1-39." Includes: Message from the Hills (Psalm 104); The Inner Circle (Matthew 26:40); Making Ready; The Vision and the Deed (Acts 16:9-12, 26:12-20); The Home Where Christ Abides; The Judgement of the Lord; The Foundation of Eternal Youth (Isaiah 40:28-31); What is a Christian?; Forbearing One Another (Ephesians 4:2); Japan and the Christian Mind of America; Christian Unity and the Mind of Christ; Saint Francis of Assisi; Matin Luther; John Wesley; David Livingstone; Turning Our Captivity (Psalm 126); The God Who Abides (Psalm 90); Paul's New Year Resolution; Men Who Heard a Voice (Acts 26:14); The Stern Risk of Living (Luke 13:22-30); The Greatest Conquest (Proverbs 16:32); The Forgiveness of Suns (Psalm 103); The Price We Pay for God's Forgiveness (Matthew 6:12, 14-15); Redeeming the Past (Romans 8:18-28, 13:8-14); The Great Friend (March 17, 1925);The Divine Friendship (John 15:5-17); Your Young Men Shall See Visions (March 19, 1925); The Victors of Christ; The Victory that Overcomes (1 John 5:4); He Came to His Own and His Own Received Him Note (John 1:11); The Cause of the Near East; Pagan vs. Christian; Idle Tales or a Living Lord (Luke 24); The Ministry of Reconciliation (II Corinthians 5:19); selection of fragments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Come Ye Apart (Mark 6:41)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: The Bread of Life (Mark 6:34-44, John 6:22-35); The Homecoming; Sail On (Philippians 3:14); An Ideal for Christian Education (Luke 2:52); God's Interpreters (I Corinthians 2:10-16); God's Mysterious Way (Genesis 44-45); Our Heart Burned Within Us (Luke 24:32); William Tyndale; The Christ Who Abides (Hebrews 13:8); How Do You Know; Can We Find Happiness?; How Make The Best of It; The Victory of Brotherhood (Race Relations Sunday); The Great Physician (Jeremiah 8:18-22; Luke 13:11-13); The Power of the Word (Hebrews 4:12-13); The Haven of the Soul; The Magnetism of the Cross (John 12:32); The Friendly Church; Giving Our Best to God (Luke 7:37-50, Mark 12:41-44); The Church and Prohibition (Amos); The Church and Modern Science (Psalm 104); The Church and Evolution; As We Pass By (Psalm 84:6); God's Broken Things (Jeremiah 18:1-4); Life's Great Choices (John 8:12); What Shall It Profit? (Mark 8:36)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "New Haven Sermons 1-66." Includes: Burden Bearers (Galatians 6:2); God's Purpose for US (Matthew 16:21); Saint Francis of Assisi; Our Great High Priest (Hebrews 4:14-16); What is Sin? (Mark 7:14-23); What Is Hell? (Matthew 5:26); What Is Judgement? (John 3:19); Leadership; What Is Salvation? (Luke 19:1-10); The Last Decisive Battle (Matthew 5:5); The Valiant Life (Ephesians 6:10-20); The Psalmist Enemies and Ours (Psalm 21); The Peace of Christmas (Luke 1:79); Good Tidings of Great Joy (Luke 2:10); The Land of Beginning Again (Numbers 13:27, Isaiah 55:6); Story Sermon: The Mater of the Inn by Robert Herrick; Four Great Days; Story-Sermon: Ethan Brand, Hawthorne; Thunder and a Voice (John 12-29); The Mansion (Blank); Young People and the Church (An Informal talk to the workers of the 1st Baptist church in Waterbury); The Second Flame (Blank); The Coming Unity (Matthew 16:3); The Prodigal Son; Making Our Tabernacles (Like 9:33); Renan's Vie de Jesus; Who Was This Man? (Mary Austin); The Man Nobody Knows (Bruce Barton); The Savior Absolute (Acts 4:11-12); The True Messiah (Matthew: A MIGHTY GOSPEL); Th Absolute Call (Luke 9:62); The Adventure of Goodness (Matthew 5:48, Genesis 17:1, Leviticus 11:45, 10:1-2, Deuteronomy 18:13); Fruits Worthy of Repentance (Luke 3:8); Who Was This Man? (John: The Eternal Savior); The Life of Christ that Can Never Be Written; "Be Still and Know That I Am God" (Psalm 46); The Christian Invincible (John 16:33); The Christ of Paul (Philippians); The Penitent Thief; Grenfell " What Christ Means to Me"; "As Far as the East" (Psalm 103:12); The Renewing of Life (Psalm 103: 4-6); Life's Amazing Contradictions (Philippians IV); The Final Harmony (Isaiah 51:11);The Amenities of the Social Life (May 2, 1927); Spiritual Reserve (Isaiah 40:28-31); A Teacher's Perspective (May 20/27, 1927); Is There a Special Providence? (Psalm 121); God's Chosen People (John 15:16); We Have Heard for Ourselves (John 4:42)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "New Haven Sermons 1-75." Includes: A Gospel of Nature (Psalm 104); The Faith of the Unbelieving (Luke 7:1-10, Matthew 8:5-13); We Shall Know (I Corinthians 13:12); As We Are Known (I Corinthians 13:12); The Price of a Cup of Water (II Samuel 23:13-17, Revelations 18:11-13); The Gospel of Cheer; The Meaning of Worship (Psalm 95:6); A Call for Pioneers (Mark 10:32); The Unanswerable Christ (Matthew 22:46); Noonday Talk to the New Haven Advertising Club; The Loyalties of a Christian (J. 6:67); Our Help and Our Deliverer (Psalm 40:17); God Our Eternal Strength (Psalm 46); Bought with a Price (I Corinthians 6:19-20); The Crisis is Past (Luke 2:11); Toyohiko Kagawa: Leader of New Japan; New Year's (John 1:50); To Pray and Not to Faint (Luke 18:1-8); Our Unseen Helpers; When All Men Speak Well (Luke 6:26); The Making of Abraham Lincoln; The Signs of a Christian (Galatians 6:17); God Seeking Us (Galatians 4:9); The God and Father of Our Lord Jesus Christ (II Corinthians 1:3); What Does God Do? (Psalm 12:1-4); Almost (Acts 26:28); In the Cross of Christ I Glory (Galatians 6:14); The Lord's Supper- a Brief Meditation; When I Survey the Wondrous Cross; If We Do Not Live Forever (I Corinthians 15:12-27); Keeping Hold on Faith (I Timothy 6:20); Good Citizenship: A Talk to Pupils in Junior High; Discovering Jesus: Talk to Older Girls' Conference (May 6, 1928; John 4:42); Salt of the Earth (Matthew 5:13); A Call for Pioneers (Mark 10:32); A Key to the Heart of God (Isaiah 49:15); Whereas I was Blind, Now I See (John 9:25); The Fear of the Lord (Psalm 111:10); The First and the Last (Genesis 1:1, Revelations 22:13, 21); The Name and the Will, Not Ashamed (Romans 1:16); Our Double Citizenship.; Zacchaeus (Luke 19); "The Life of Christ Constraineth Us" (II Corinthians 5:14)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "New Haven Sermons K, Islesboro 20 & 21 & 22." Includes: Knock & It Shall Be Open Unto You; What Do You Really Want? (Matthew 20:21); Pray Without Ceasing (I Thessalonians 5:17); Jesus' Communion With the Father (Matthew 14:23); Lest We Forget (Psalm 137); The Lord's Song in a Strange Land (Psalm 137:4); Our Help and Our Deliverer (Psalm 40:17); A Review of the Week Past in Our Church; Ye Must Be Born Again (John 3); Is It Ever Too Late?; God's Strange Ways with Men (Isaiah 55:8); God Looks Down Upon His World?; Having Found Yet Seeking (I Corinthians 3:11); The Home Where Christ Comes to Stay (Luke 10:38-42); The Old Fight (I Timothy 6:12); A Call for Pioneers (Mark 10:32); Harmer John (Hugh Walpole); We Bear The Name of Christian (Acts 11:26); Abraham Lincoln (02/10/1929); A Singular Life (Elizabeth Stuart Philips); What Does It Mean to Be a Christian; The Second Mile (Matthew 5:41); The Bread of the Supper (03/24/1929); Life Overcoming Death (Easter, John 10:10); What Did They Do With the Spices? (Easter); The Lure of the Trail; The Grace of Humility (Philippians 25:11); "Seek Ye First..." (Matthew 6:33); What Can King's Daughters Do to Stimulate Growth in Membership; When Spring Comes in the Heart (Ecclesiastes 12:1); A Christian and His Life-Work; Thine Hand Upon Me (Psalm 139:5); The Enjoyment of God (Psalm 43:4); With Christ on the Trail.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: N. Islesboro Sermons: Includes: After the Smile, a Still Small Voice (I Kings 19:12); The Kiss of Judas; They Crucified Him; John 14:9a; "What Have We to do with Thee Jesus of Nazareth?"; Seek Ye Faith (Matthew 6:33); "He that hath seen Me hath the Father" (John 14:9b); Mark 6:56b; "If Jehovah be God, follow Him; But if Baal, then follow Him" (I Kings 18:21); My Peace I Give Unto You (John 14:27); 'I cam that they may have life and may have it abundantly (John 10:10); Amos 3:2; Are We By Nature Good or Bad?; The Sword of Christ and the Angel's Song; 'Except ye become as little children ye cannot enter into the Kingdom of Heaven" (Matthew 18:3); Friendship (John 15 1-15, I Corinthians 18, Romans8:35-39); What Is a Christian?; Prayer (Pray Without Ceasing); Immortality (John 2: 25); The Pleasures of Life; Funeral: Islesboro (08/28/1920, I Peter I: 24-25, John 14, I Corinthians 15: 55-58); Boy, Girl, Woman, Man; Endure Hardness as a Good Soldier of Jesus Christ (II Timothy 2:1-15, 3:11-12, 4:1-8); Follow Me! (Matthew 4:17-18); The Temptation of Jesus (Matthew 4:1-11); The Things That Count; High Ideals; Funeral (07/29/1920, John 14, I Corinthians 15:55-58); "To them that love God, all things work together for good"; Why I Am a Christian? ; The Divine Commonplace (1 Kings 19:12); The Answer to Phillip

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "New Haven Sermons L", 1-53. Includes: The Sympathy of Christ (Hebrews 5:2); Our Double Citizenship (Matthew 6:10, Philippians 3:20); Workers Together with God (I Corinthians 3:9); Midas or The Golden Touch; The Old Fight (I Timothy 6:12); The Lure of the Trail; The Glory of the Ordinary (Genesis 28:16); The Greater Empire (Matthew 8:1-13, Revelation 7:9-17); The Responsible Life (Luke 9:23); The Priesthood of All Believers (I Peter 2:1-10); "For Their Sakes..." (John 17:19); What Will You Do With Jesus? (Matthew 27:22); A Christian Gift to the Heavenly Father (Proverbs 23:26); In Quietness and In Confidence (Isaiah 30:15); The River of God (Psalm 65:9); Faithful in the Least (Luke 16:10); The Flight of James Carker (Dombrey & Son); Your Wys Are Not My Ways (Isaiah 55); The Story of Fr. Cristoforo (Manzoni's 'The Betrothed'); Crime and Punishment (Feodor Dostoevsky); I Am The Way; The Call of Home; I Am The Truth (John 14:6); I Am The Life (John 14:6); The Real and Living Way (John 14:6); The Comfort & The Challenge of the Gospel (John 14:27, Matthew 10:34); The Silence That Follows the Storm (I Kings 19, Elijah); The Spiritual Sense (Psalm 34:8); Something More (Luke 7:46); The Gospel of the Birds (Songs 2:11-12); The Mirror (John 17:3); Confession and Counsel (04/08/1930); What Will You Do With Jesus?; The Infinite Background (Holy Week & Easter); He Always Comes Back (Acts 2:24); Sanctuary (I Corinthians 3:16); Holy Holy Holy (Isaiah 6:3); Help Thou My Unbelief (Mark 9:24); If I Should Begin My Ministry Again (05/22/1930, Yale Divinity School Seniors); Release (Luke 4:18); A Faith So Deep as Life (Amos 5:21-24, Jeremiah 2:20-22, 7:1-7, 13:22-27, Isaiah 29); One Thing Thou Lackest (Mark 10:21)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "New Haven Sermons M." Includes: "This Do In Remembrance of Me"; The Wonder of It All (Mark 10:32); Junior Sermon: Red Fingers (Wellesley College); The Gospel of the Mountains (Psalm 121:1); The High Moments of Life (Matthew 17:1-20); Seeing Straight (Talk to the Girl scouts of Shelton-Derby, John 9); Saint Augustine (1500th Anniversary of Death, 08/24/1930); Our Pleasant Unity in Christ (Psalm 133:1); The Narrow Gate to Life (Luke 13:24, Matthew 7:14); The Right Place (Luke 24:49); A Christian Morale for Hard Times (Romans 12); The Finisher of Our Faith (Hebrews 12:2); A Call for Pioneers (Mark 10:32); God The Eternal Torment of Men (Hebrews 4:13); Making Our Image of God (Psalm 50:21, Genesis 1:24-31; Matthew 5); Fair Lady Charity (I Corinthians 13); The Life of Faith as the Moral Equivalent of War (I Timothy 6:12); The Old Futility and The Eternal Hope (Psalm 42); Alone with God (John 8:1-11); Come! (Revelation 22:17); Junior Sermon; Children's Sermon; Junior Sermon: "What If We Lose a Race? (I Corinthians 9:24-27); Thru The Roof (Junior Sermon, Mark 2:1-12); The Cleansing That Is Christmas (Psalm 51:7); Redeeming The Past (Genesis 45:8); Christ & The Ages of Man (Hebrews 13:8); "Like A s A Father Pitieth" (Psalm 103); The April of Self-Pity (Psalm 37-38); The Call to a Christian Future (02/01/1931); The Single Eye (Matthew 6:23); Our Active & Watchful God (Jeremiah 1:12); Potter & Clay (Isaiah 64:8); The One Sure Test of a Christian (Matthew 7:20); Traffic Lights in the City of God; He Ever Lives & Intercedes (Hebrews 7:25); Meditation on the Cross for Good Friday Service (Calvary Church, 04/03/1931), Easer Morning Communion (1931); If a Man Die Shall He Live Again? (Job 14:14); The World-Renounce or Reclaim? (Matthew 6:10); The Crooked Way & The Straight (Isaiah 59:8); My Own Faith (Romans 2:16, Philippians 3:8); The Call of Home (12/07/1930); The Glory of Failing (Matthew 10:39); Be Still and Know (Psalm 46:10); What Is Man? (Psalm 8, Job 7, Hebrews 2); What Is Man? II. The Modern Problem; The Humility of the 8th Psalm; What Is Man? III. Christ's Answer; Remember Jesus Christ (I Corinthians 11:24); The Christian Conscience & The State: The Macintosh Case (Daniel 3); The Lilies of the Field (Matthew 6:25-24); The Wonder of Our Faith (Mark 10:32); Enemies & Allies (Luke 9:49-50); Psalm Sunday Union Communion (03/29/1931).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Sermon Outlines." Includes: Faith in Time of Confusion (Mark 4:40, Psalm 71:1); I Would Have Told You (John 14:2); The Supernatural Community (Philippians 3:20); The Baptism of Jesus (Matthew 3:13-17); A Ten-Year Plan for Peace; Paid-Up Capital (Matthew 6:19); The Spiritual Sense By Which We Find God Real (Yale Chapel, 11/19/1931); A Faith Deep as Life (Yale Chapel, 11/20/1931, Jeremiah 2:22, 13:23); Thank God (Psalm 92); Guidance (Isaiah 58:11); The Guidance of Christ's Flock (Exodus 13:22); God So Loved The World (Christmas, John 3:16); The Signs of the Times (Matthew 16:3); Commit Thy Way Unto The Lord... (New Year, 1932, Psalm 37:5); The Kingdom of God (Zechariah 8:1-8); The Imitation of Christ (John 13:15); In Jesus' Footprints (Luke 9:23); Bridge Builders, Road Makers... (II Corinthians 5:20, Matthew 5:9); Taking Account of the Stock of the Soul (Romans 8); If I Forgot (Psalms 137:5); The Breastplate of Righteousness (Ephesians 6:14); Washington The Man; Jesus Christ and a Young Man's Needs (Matthew 6:33); The Lure of the Trail; For Christ & The Church (Ephesians 3:21, Luke 7:36-38, John 21:15-17); The Cost of the World's Redemption is the 'fellowship of His sufferings' (Philippians 3:10); The Best We Have to Give (Acts 3:6); Confirming God's Choice (II Peter 1:10); The Lost Art of Contemplation (05/01/1932); The Education of the Heart (Luke 10:27); God's Pity For His Children (Psalm 103); Junior Sermon: Steeples from East Rock; Growing Up; Junior Sermon: Guidance (I Kings 12); Junior Sermon: Girded Loins & Burning Lights (Luke 12:35); Junior Sermon: Marry Slessor. I.; Junior Sermon: 'Bear ye one another's burdens' (Galatians 6); The Sense of Mission (John 18:37, Acts 1:8); The Jews (Interfaith Conference at Colby College, 01/09/1936); Mark's Children's Sermon; Looking Unto Jesus (Hebrews 12:2).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "O Sermon Outlines." Includes: Henry Martyn (Gethsemane Souls); What I Owe to Christ (C.J. Andrews Autobiography); The Great Soul of Walter Scott (Centenary of his death, Isaiah 32:2); No Going Back (Exodus 14-15); The Goodness & Severity of God (Romans 11:22); The Green Bay Tree (Psalm 37:35-36); The Life I Might Have Lived (I Samuel 18:10-11, Matthew 25:1-13); Jerusalem the Golden (All Souls Communion, Revelation 22:1-5); Road-Makers: A Sermon for Armistice Sunday (Isaiah 40:3); You Can Never Pay Your Debts (Thanksgiving, Romans 1:1-16); God Can Use A Few (I Samuel 14:6, Judges 7:1-7); 'Two or Three- In My Name' (Matthew 18:20); Christ & The American Home (Colossians 3:12-21); The Bethany Home of Jesus (Luke 10:38-42, Mark 11:11, John 11:12); Jesus & The Child-Heart (Christmas, Matthew 18:3); Tending the Garden of the Soul (Proverbs 4:23); Know Thyself (Psalm 139); Master Thyself (Proverbs 16:32, Romans 1:1); Give Thyself; Forget Thyself (Exodus 34:29); The Spirit of the New Missions-Sharing (Ecclesiastes 11:1); Jesus & Our Daily Bread (Matthew); Jesus & The Good Things of Life (Matthew 6:33); Jesus & our Human Relations (Matthew 18: 21-35); Jesus & The Heart's Peace (John 14-27); Learning to Wait for God (Psalm 130:6-7); Waiting For God (Lamentations 3:26); The Growing Life (Business Girl's Club at YWCA 02/23/1933); Security (Psalm 4:8); Confession (Psalm 32:5); Three Ways of Following Jesus; He Had to Come Back (Acts 2:24); The Gaiety of Holiness (Philippians 4:4); 'Filled with the Knowledge of His Will' (Colossians 1:9); Labels: Conservative, Liberal, Radical; Men Who Heard a Voice; Theme: What is Life?', Religion is Life, I am The Life; Who Will Speak for Me?; Your Labor is Not in Vain (I Corinthians 15:58); The Psalmist's Enemies and Ours (Psalm 27); How Shall We Hold the Faith? (II Timothy 1:12-14); Jesus & The Good Things in Life; Junior Sermon: Our Daily Servants; The Little Lame Prince (Sir Walter Scott, 09/18/1932); Junior Sermon: Artist Reynolds on the Platform; Tending the Garden of the Soul; On Peace; The Adventure of Teaching; Setting the Lord Before Us (Psalm 16:8); This World or the Next?; Isolation as Hell Fellowship as Heaven; An Easter Prayer; Letter to Diffie by Alice; The Irrational Nature of Man; Kindness to Animals; Doing Good: The Glory of Life (Acts 10:38).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "New Haven Sermons: P." Includes: The Fundamentals of Recovery (Psalm 127:1); Discipline in the Christian Life (I Corinthians 9:24-27); When Duty Becomes Pleasure (Genesis 29:20); David and Araunah (II Samuel 24:24); God's Way With Sin & Sinners Series: We Are Sinners (Romans 3), We Cannot Save Ourselves, God is Ready to Help (Psalm 46:1), He Was Numbered with the Transgressors (Mark 15:28); Cloud of Witnesses (Hebrews 12:1-2); The Trumpet of Joab; Jesus Christ & The Forgotten Man (Luke 19:10); Infinite Possibilities (Matthew 17:20); Keep Knocking (Luke 11:9); Sons & Brothers (1 John 4:11); The Shepherd-Heart of Jesus (Matthew 9:36); The Donkey: Children's Story for Palm Sunday; Right Hand of Fell; Junior Sermon (Matthew 7:24-27, 09/10/1933); The Christian Home (Talk to Calvary Mother's Club, 10/05/1933); Eternal Life (Romans 6:4); The Bible in London, Codex Sinaiticus (Psalm 119:111); The Gospel of Mark (Mark 1:1); The Gospel of Matthew; The Gospel of Luke; Thunder & A Voice (John 12:29); The Gospel of John (John 20:31); King on a Donkey (Matthew 21:5); The Inner Shrine (03/20/1934); A Faith Deep as Life (Jeremiah 13:23); Thy Home That Is to Be (Y.P.S., 03/06/1934); A Mother's Ambition (Matthew 20:20-28); We Must Tell the Good News (Colossians 4:3-4); Discipline- Our Part (I Corinthians 9:24-27); Creators of Beauty (Psalm 98); Revealers of Truth (Hebrews 1:1-2); Noble Spirits (Ecclesiastes 44, Matthew 5:13-16); Junior Sermon: Halloween Season; Jesus Christ Our Motive & Our Goal (Luke 7:36-38, 47-50; Praise Him With the Organ (Psalm 150:5); The Rule of the Christian Athlete (I Corinthians 9:25); The Gospel of the Eternal Traveler; In Jesus' Footprints (Luke 9:23).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Q: Waterville." Includes: Skillful Teachers (10/09/1934); Launch Out Into The Deep (Luke 5:4); The Right Place (Luke 24:49); The Courage of Daniel (Daniel 6:1-10); God In American History; Christ Wants All the World (Matthew 28:19-20); Our Pleasant Unity in Christ (Psalm 133:1); When Will The Kingdom Come? (Revelation 22:20); The Lord is My Shepherd (Psalm 23); Mrs. Nobody of London; Sleeping in Church (Acts 20:7-12); We Need to Worship (Psalm 95:6); The Sting & the Healing of the Gospel (Matthew 11:28); Three Adjectives: Secular, Religious, Christian (Matthew 7:21); The Antichrist & Nationalism (II Thessalonians 2:3-4); Protestant & Catholic (Luke 9:49-50); Surrender (Luke 18:22); What Is The Battleground (Today)? (Ephesians 6:12); The Weapons of Our Warfare (I Samuel 17:32-49); The Discipline of the Christian Warrior (Ephesians 6:10-18); The Inner Shrine (Revelation 3:20); The Outcome of Our Warfare (Ephesians 6:13); "Be Still and Know that I am God" (04/07/1935); The Devils are Cast Off (Acts 26:18); Breakfast on the Shore (John 21); Put On Christ (Galatians 3:27); I Press On (Philippians 3:12&14); Where Does Peace Stand? Niebuhr & Liberalism; This World or the Next? (Matthew 5, Philippians 3:20); Memorial Day Address (05/30/1935); Can We Be Christian Today? (based on talk from 09/09/1930); The Gospel of the Birds (Songs 2:11-12); The Outer World & The Inner; The Supernatural Community; 'Our Citizenship is in heaven' (Philippians) The True Other-Worldliness; The World Renounce or Reclaim.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "R1 Waterville." Includes: Chesterton: Christian Poet; Trying to Justify Ourselves (Luke 10:29); From Slavery to Freedom (Luke 4:18, Romans 7:24-25); A Franciscan Pilgrimage (09/1935); Mayflower Hill Freshmen Vesper (09/29/1935); Victims or Victors (Romans 8:36-37); Salvation Individual or Social (Luke 19:9-10); The Life & Sunshine of my house has been extinguished; God Shall Judge the Nations (Armistice 1935); Something More (Luke 7:36-50, Matthew 5:38-48); The Church's Declaration of Independence (Farmington Men's Supper, 10/22/1935); What We Expect to Those Who Join (Baptism); The Present (Watch- Night Service 1935-36); Great Books I Have Read in 1935; Tolstoy's My Confession; Vine and Branches (John 15); Why I Am A Minister (01/19/1936); The Kingdom Come-In My Church; Communism & Our Christian Faith; Adventures in Conciliation (Matthew 5); The Church Within the Church (Luke 12:32, 03/01/1936); Is It the Same Gospel? (Hebrews 13:8); Natural Disasters & The Providence of God (03/1936); Even Me (Luke 23:42); Song & Victory (II Chronicles 20:21-22); New Haven: 04/21/1936; Cultivating Growth in Christian Character (01/21/1936); An Ideal for Christian Education (Luke 2:52); The Price of Liberty (Acts 22:28, I Peter 2:16);

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sermon outlines with index "the preacher and the teacher trying to live in the same [?] of clothes" (p.2); with notes from 1972-1973

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Writings are divided into three subseries: Publications, Church Pamphlets, and Addresses. Over the course of Brush's career, he wrote a series of short books and sermons that were printed as books and church pamphlets. These materials include manuscripts and printed additions of Brush's publications, and copies of church pamphlets authored and distributed by Brush. In the final subseries are numerous speeches given by Brush at various occasions outside of church events.

did.unittitle: Stroundwater Days: Reminiscences of the Inner and Outer Life Original Manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written while a Pastor at the Village Churches. Includes a letter to his mother concerning the manuscript.

did.unittitle: Stroundwater Days: Reminiscences of the Inner and Outer Life Bound Reproduction

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Intended for publication and distribution.

The Door: A Message to Persons Becoming Protestants

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Andover Newton Waymarks. 3 Copies: One addressed to Jule from "Dad", another with an annotated date, and one pristine copy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Christian Conscience and the State (1930); Colby's Commencement Dinner (1956); The Difference You Make to God (May 29, 1927); Five Prayer Meeting Talk March 4, 1930); How the Light Comes; If I Should Begin my Ministry Again (May 22, 1930); Inadequacies of Seniors to Give Witness Today; Is it Possible to be a Christian Today? (Sept. 9, 1930); The Macintosh Citizenship Case (1930); The Marxist Challenge to the Church (April 15, 1934); Mexico; The Mind of Dean Page

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Obeying God's Commands (Aug. 29, 1933); Preaching the Old Gospel in a Challenging Age (Oct. 21, 1924); The Rise of Modern Religious Ideas (1930s?); Sanderson of Oundle (Oct. 4, 1936); Sin—Its Cause and Cure (April 4, 1933); The Technique of Worship (Sept. 27, 1929); Testament of Youth (April 22, 1934); The Theology of the Poets (1930s?); What to Preach (Feb. 12, 1934); Thy Will be Done (April 2, 1926); Young People on Worship (Oct. 1930); Why Missions and Why China? (1930s?)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John W. Brush was a disciplined writer and he kept journals and diaries of nearly every year of his life. His journals are split into three subseries: Personal Journals, Reading Notes, and Diaries. The personal journals include thoughts, poems, notes, and reflections relevant to his personal and academic life, as well as to current national and global events such as presidential elections, political events, global conflicts, and world wars. Some of these notebooks, in particular the Business Year Book journals, include data and information pertinent to the year the journal was printed such as postage prices, flag rules, notable inventions, populations of major cities, the seven wonders of the world, legal holidays in various states, and more. The Reading Notes are Brush's notes and ponderings concerning works he has read and research he has done. The last subseries is Diaries which includes dozens of by-year daily calendars of Brush's appointments, calls, and daily activities.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift of Walton, Christmas 1914. Important events listed on page 366: "The War" and "The Lusitania Disaster"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inside pages include calendars, wage tables, City Populations, Easter Sundays, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "I." This the first in a series labeled with roman numerals. This journal was kept by Brush while in Plattsburg, N.Y. at the S.A.T.C. Camp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "I" Waterville, Maine and Mount Vernon, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entitled "Miscellaneous Notes and Extracts: II" This journal includes a unique bookmark.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "My First Parish," and it outlines his first summer in Islesboro.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This journal was a gift from John's sister Agnes to him at Christmas. Brush entitled this journal, "Journal of Thoughts and Observations II."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This journal was written in while Brush lived in Newton Center, Mass. It includes later additions from November 1979 through January 1980.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "III"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "V"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "VI"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "VII"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entries from Portland, Maine and New Haven, CT.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "England: Vol. I"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "England: Vol. II"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "Lit 3," and it includes self-authored poems and music mostly.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "With Two Tramps," with entries spanning travels across New England. This journal have later additions.

did.unittitle: Book Manuscript of Personal Journal Entries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JWB Inside cover note: "mss fragments for a book with materials drawn from his journal and a letter from Douglas MacIntosh about it Jan. 19, 1930. The second mss seems to have been written ca. 1939," dates of journal entries compiled range from 12/06/1923-10/15/1927

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "My Travels," detailing his trip through Europe. This journal was a gift from a 'Miss. Miller.'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "Journal of Our Journey in the Province of Quebec." The end of this journal has a note from Brush in June 10, 1966.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "Poetry V."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "Poems of 15 Years," all written during his time in Waterville, Maine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This journal was used during Brush's time in Waterville, Maine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This journal catalogs the events of the beginning of World War II. Many entries on relevant dates and reflections on notable events.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This journal has scattered dates (some from 1937), articles, and reflections from later in 1977-78.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Most entries are from 1940 or 1941 during JWB's time on a farm in New Hampshire. At the end, there are two reflection entries from 1978.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This journal has more entries during and about World War II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "Journal of a Tour." It includes entries Brush made while traveling through Virginia, West Virginia, Mexico, and California.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Special entry concerning President Truman on October 25, 1952

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This journal details more of John W. Brush's travels through Strasbourg and other parts of Europe. This journal includes many of Brush's sketches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a reflection by Brush in 1978.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is an art pad entitled "Istanbul Paintings," filled with watercolor paintings of views found in his travels of Istanbul.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "Journal of Our Sabbatical Leave," which includes entries about Brush's travels in Europe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a miscellaneous travel journal, with entries detailing trips to Spain, Morocco, Portland, Oregon, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brush entitled this journal, "Poetry 4," with later reflections from 1965 and 1966.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This journal includes details of John Brush's trip to Puerto Rico.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entitled: "Pilgrimage Spain," this journal outlines John Brush's missionary trip to Spain. This journal includes an ending reflection by Brush written on June 23, 1974.

did.unittitle: "Glistening Sands: Proverbs, Sayings, Epigrams, Couplets, etc." That Have Given Me Entertainment and Guidance
"Lit 2: Notes on Literature and Extracts from the Poets"
"Philosophia: Jottings on Philosophical Subjects with Theological Content"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newton Center, Mass.

did.unittitle: Bibliotheque Nationale et Universitaire de Strasbourg Library Card

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Valuable Notes from Selbie: The Psychology of Religion; Pater: Extracts from Huneker's Essay; Truth, Sincerity; Worship; Notes from Bromberg: The Mind of Man; Dates and Notes in Prot. Missions, etc.; Notes from E.C. Moore: The Spread of Christianity in the Modern; World; Notes from Our Economic Morality and the Ethic of Jesus etc. (by Harry F. Ward)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: English Prose Style (by Herbert Style); R.L.S; My Temperament and How I can Change it; John Ruskin; Levine Lenin, Russia, etc.; R. B. Merriman: Suleiman the Magnificent

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes from Henry Lodge's "George Washington" etc.; Worship; Von Vogt's "Modern Worship"; Life of John Patterson; Chronology; Francis Thompson; Newman; Richard Hutton: Cardinal Newman; Man the Master

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on Brooks Beeches Lectures, etc.; Bonhoeffer: Letters and Papers from Prison; Douglas: Nonviolent Crass; James Baldwin; Manning: Essays in Orthodox Dissent; Chesterton: Everlasting Man; Preper: Leisure the Basis of Culture; Freedom and Authority in our Time; Milder; Minear: Second Coming; Hayan: Counter Renaissance; C.C. Morrison: The Unfinished Reformation; Garrison: A Protestant Manifesto; Casserly: Retreat from Christianity; Muelder

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Barth: Deliverance to the Captive; Evelyn Underhill: Anthology of the Love of God; String Fellow: A Second Birth; J.H. Plumb: The Death of the Past; Jas W. North: History of Augusta; Piety and Justice; Rebecca West: Court and Castle; Hobhouse: Autobiography; Huxley: Devils of London; Allan Chalmers: High Wind at Noon; From State Church to Pluralism; Trevelyan: An Autobiography and Other Essays; Mazzini: The Black-robed Monk of Modern Nationalism; Chamberlain: The World's Iron Age; C.C. Morrison

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Royce; Davis Robinson; Clutton Brock; Cammaerts: Flower of Grass; Bixler; Education of Henry Adams; Streeter Reality; Review of Mrs. Barnett's "Canon Barnett"; Pringle Pattison; Adams: Popular Psychology; E.V. Lucas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Masters on Lindsay; Nells; Barrie; Paul Verghese: The Joy of Freedom; Paul Shepard: Man in the Landscape; R.L. Stevenson: Travels in a Donkey; Yevtushenko: Stolen Apples; Gorainoff: God's Mayfaran; Walt Wagoner: Say A Good Word for Jesus; Yogi Commissar Ambassador; Harvey Cox: The Feast of Fools; T.S.R. Boose: Death in the Middle Ages; John Jowles: The French Lieutenants Lady; Symbols of Evangelists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rilke; Dorothy L. Sayers: A Matter of Eternit; Alland Hunter: Secretly Armed; Seven Pillars of Wisdom; W.H. Clarke: Psychedelic Ecstasy; The Scandal of Silence; Brian Vachon: A Time to be Born; Hans Habe: The Wounded Land; Graham Greene: A Sort of Life; Eliade's; YMCA; Sweeney: Realms

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Payot; Jean Christopher; Floyd Schmoe: A Year in Paradise; Chas Davis: A Question of Conscience; Stanley Romaine Hopper: The Crisis of Faith; Embracing the Christmas Oratorio; Skyargoard: One in Christ; A Catalogue of Series; Notes on Lorenz on Aggression; Life Against Death; Buchan; We Beheld his Glory; Mumford: Faith for Living

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Munford: Condition of Man; Dixon: The Human Situation; Orton; Northrop; W.O. Douglas: Of Men and Mountains; I Promessi Sposi; Kristellon: Penaiss That; Soby: Roualt; Harnack; Brion's Durer; Denifs Sutton: Triumphant Satyr; Littell; Vogt: Road to Survival; Harvey Cox: The Secular City; Irving Singer: The Natures of Love

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Victor Hallanz: My Dear Timothy; The Committee of 100 in Support of NAACP; Robs Paine: Hubris; Macgregor: The Vatican Revolution; Murdock; Albright Phillips Brooks; Littell: The German Phoenix; Rupp: Principalities and Powers; Davies: The Two Humanities; H.C. Jackson: Man Reaches out to God; Jean Danielon: The Scandal of Truth; Cochrane: Christianity and Classical Culture; Musurillo: Symbolism and the Christian Imagination; Colin Wilson: The Outsider; Stringfellow: A Private and Public Faith

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: G.G. Coutton's Autobiography; Jingley: STM Thesis; Heroes of That; Becker; Watkin: The Church in Council; P. Hughes: The Church in Crises; R. Reynolds: Sinner of Saint Ambrose; Howard Spring and Another Thing; Morris Cohen: The Faith of a Liberal; Drucker: The End of Economic Man; Dorothy Day: The Long Loneliness; Koestler: The Yogi and the Commissor; Coulton: Fourscore; Honest to God; W.E. Hocking: Strength of Man and Nations; Sweeney: The Knowledge Explosion; W.L. Bradley: The Man and his Work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Martimeau: Endeavors After the Church Life; Mumford: The Transformations of Man; J.H. Holmes: I Speak for Myself; Galloway: The Cosmic Christ; Briton's Ideas and Men; Butterfield: Christianity in European History; Nichols: Democracy and the Christians; Lamprecht: Our Religious Traditions; Nash: The University in the Modern World; Podo's Sriel; Peguy; Helen White: Social Criticism; Dorothy Musgrove Arnold: Collected by Christ to Heal

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: B. Berennson: Rumor and Reflections; Tra Angelico; Oxford Book of English Mystical Verse; Charles Williams: The Descent of the Dove; Ecclesia Spiritualis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: How did it Work?; Imagining; When the Curtain Falls; John Passon: Thomas Jefferson; Carlyle: French Revolution; Gerald Heard: Preface to Prayer; Margaret Mordecai: Indian Dream Lands; Journal April 1933: Review of Bury's Idea of Progress; Baptist Chronicle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
